folder 02 - 034
It is not enough to DISMISS time, space, causality + the phenomenal world as irreal; two questions remain: 1) If they are irreal, why do we experience them? Why should an organism arise that perceives things which don't exist? 2) What is real? It cannot actually be said that we can't know something; all that can be said is that we don't know how to know it (I am thinking of Kant's Dinge-an-sich), no one can claim to know what can't be known. (Note) Regarding (1): we are locked into this irreal structure by Zebra, which creates it to maintain organization + functioning of its parts. Zebra is not interested in making us aware of our actual situation but only in organization + functioning; these irreal elements are essentially expedients - the criterion is use (i.e. a pragmatic one) not veracity, activity not knowledge. Regarding (2): see preceding 10,000 pages. The Kantian unknowable can berhaps be discerned [...] detected by the perturbations it creates in the phenomenal world. If it creates none (but it does) probably it is not there. What we need to do is follow Hume's careful scrutinizing method, not German double abstractions based on Hindu assetions about the ultimate unknowability of reality behing appearance. (Note) To preserve us as functioning parts it is preserving our integrity, our health + our physical [nee]ds; the deluding serves our biological but not our purely intellectual needs; the first outweighs the 2nd.

analysis, urges us to the conclusion that in this sentence "appear" carries the force of "give a false impression." I'm sorry, but that is the way I asses "appear" in this linguistic context. In fact I am positive of it. In that case, if that is accepted as a given, then: AB = C rather than AB = C So: we have AB which equals C. If C = T, then C = T. So AB = T in this context (formally). USA 1974 + Rome CAD 45 = AB. So USA 1974 + Rome CAD 45 = T + not T as would seem. By the law of the excluded middle I get AB indicating T rather than merely failing to indicate T (i.e. it is either T or T. This is all based on equating "appear" with "give a false impression"; viz: AB gives a false impression of C which = T. Therefore AB gives a false impression of T. "False impression" = T. What other interpretation can there be? In those cases (which I presume do exist) where AB does = C, then in those cases AB = T, but this is not an instance of that. To go from logic to empiricism. I say we're dealing with a very high order of duplicity here: in magnitude, in power, in meaning, in importance. Since normally AB = C + C always = T, then an inversion of one of the two fundamentals of Kantian ontological

I don't think I see the world as other people do; I presume what I'm trying to prove in this whole Exegesis, namely a medieval realist worldview in which the sensible world-order is [unconsciously] assumed to be printed out from an upper more real realm - this is also the world of the shaman: two level reality, as found in Plato but repudiated by Aristotle. 2-3-74 was a coming into full consciousness -+ control- of this worldview. My leap to full abstracting in 2-74 was an epiphany of - some archaic -probably medieval- substrate in me, bursting through the thin rationalist modern veneer. It was anamnesis all right (v. p 512, Plato's view of how we apprehend universals) but - Primitive is the word I want. Contemporary philosophy makes no sense to me. & yet, as Pat Warrick says, I write from the standpoint of quantum mechanics. My God isn't even transcendent - No; I don't believe in heaven or an afterlife. What do I believe in? Kantian phenomenology? That an invisible latent realm exists immanently in the sensible world or that no objective world in itself exists at all? Acosmism? Something Eastern (e.g. Brahmanism?) "If you press world hard enough it yields up God" - paraphrase of p. 485. "I define God as world under the threat of death ... God forced into the open, + put to work in the service of evading death." I am not I; I am not here; I am not now. Buddhist idealism? No, because when self + world go, God -not the void- replaces them, + world is seen as an abstraction outside space + time, an interconnected system of ideas in His mind; more precisely, a memory [s...t .n] process (i.e. not static).

E-33

Just consider this all in terms of the Kantian ontological ordering categories: the way (space, time + causation) our minds structure world. Then my whole model is not a scientific theory about world but Dasein itself, a way of being in world (in the Kantian sense). As to how new + radical it is, let me ask, how many worldviews (Dasein) omit causation entirely (!) + replace it with my "binary elective decision-making choosing" by a sentient, volition, self-regulating (perpetually choosing) system? That is temporally disjunctive - which virtually converts time totally into space (spatial "frames")? So my model is a new way-of-being-in-the-world, not just an explanation, theoretical + intellectual! My model is, in fact, a verbal description of a new way I found of Dasein! The modem that we project (causation) to explain process (+ explain it inadequately, as Malebranche was the first to realize) is the source of our enslavement, because it is the ontological structuring "causation" that is necessity, ananke, determinism, etc. Here precisely is what literally blinds us + binds us, our own modem: it reduces the universe of switching (enantiodromia as perpetual decision-making choice) to blind necessity, + engenders the tyranny of destructive time.

before. I mean, just think what it would mean vis-a-vis our way of perceiving/understanding world if we ceased to utilize space or time as a Kantian ordering/structuring category? And in fact when the utilization of causation ceases, our sense of time is drastically altered (time sharply diminishes), and our sense of space drastically is altered (as I figure it, time is converted into space, so we get a great diminution in the time factor and a great augmentation in the spatial factor); but, most of all, introduced as a totally new factor is an apperception of the flicker pulsation in which the system (reality) switches on and off, as well as the binary forking decision-making; the totality of all this is that very simply our occlusion lifts and we are in another world entirely, a world I identify with the Garden. And this really could not have happened before this decade, what with computers, new theories about information, modern physics, etc. It is just now beginning to happen. And no one --no one!-- has seen the involvement of Pauline Christian mysticism, that in fact this is the pay-off ingredient. And this would explain why for over seven years I have alternated between believing Christ has returned and believing that I had evolved some kind of ultra-modern worldview connected with physics and epistemology, etc.

Okay; I have one final thing to say and herewith I rest my case, triumphantly. My binary forking, which I have already said is an indeterminate element entering what always before was conceived of as causality (under various names, such as astral determinism): what is this if not the "two slit" phenomenon familiar in subatomic physics, which is the very essence of the indeterminate factor in reality! It is known to us scientifically only on a subatomic level. Yet I say (I think I say) I have perceived this as the very basis of reality per se, the reality process of change, of flux, of all cause-and-effect at all levels, micro and macro. What I have been calling "binary forking choosing" is simply the "two-slit indeterminate phenomenon" but at a larger level, and it is a level that embraces all change. I am saying, some kind of mentational volitional sentient mind or mindoid entity --perhaps that of the total system itself-- has some kind of steering or governing involvement

the modem we would see the procession of discrete, differing frames that have no effect on each other, + this perception would either allow us or compel us to investigate the situation in order to understand it (i.e. precisely what I saw in 3-74 that I termed Valis or "pretextual cause"). Immediately we would comprehend reality as a single unitary field + not plural objects bumping into one another. After that, God knows what we'd conclude. It's taken me seven years of hard work to come up with this "binary forking" model, + I was able to do it only because Valis entered my mind in 3-74 + caused me to apprehend the dialectic as primary + irreducable, + then, recently, disclosed (1) the procession of discrete "time frames" + then (2) the initial zero firing. I never could have developed a model on my own! Based on what I saw. Well, I guess this is why we employ the modem of causality as an ontological Kantian ordering category. It seems logical (because in fact it is a conceptual tautology! It actually tells us nothing). (But this precisely is why we have philosophy: to analyze + demolish such circular lines of thought.) (+, precisely, this is why normal people have no use for philosophy; they see no problem; they construe philosophy as creating a problem, a false problem, not solving a real one. Hence Michael Bishop's rejection of the worth of my preoccupations.) So back in the late Forties I figured

has changed. The percipient now stands, as it were, in the relation to the world that God does. (This sounds like the Hermetic transform, achieved through the alchemical wedding, the union of opposites from which the hierogamy occurs with the birth of the "Golden Flower" - hierogamy leading to the divine child known, too, to the Taoist alchemists. Viewed this way, the "Golden Flower" is God occurring in -as?- the person's own mind, + the divine illumination is related to this mysterious birth. Is this what Jesus meant by being "born in the spirit"? + is this new symbiotic life form Christ? (Back to Augustine! We have come full circle. The secret seems to be: world is found in the person's own mind, but it is world radically reinterpreted; we must abandon all conventional ideas of what "world" is. This is world in a mind: the person's mind; therefore world is a structure of intelligible ideas related to Pythagoras' Kosmos but not material. Since space + causation are abolished, it is literally possible for this structure to be in the person (as if he is Adam Kadmon filling all space; i.e. the universe; but this is due to the fact that "space" is a Kantian ontological ordering category.) Knowledge of this structure is a priori + absolute. It is like with a hologram wherein the picture is intact at any given piece however small. The percipient is no longer a percipient at all, outside reality with only an a posteriori contingent knowledge of the totality. He knows by the means that God knows: a priori + necessarily. This structure so known is coherent + intact, consisting of

9

O-1

Reality — inc. the percipient as part to whole — is experienced as one, unitary, interested structure (Which is "true kosmos" as Pythagoras used the term; hence "an extinct true kosmos & it's still there.") Reality valued this way — 5D & a single continuum — is such that "the void" (mēden) no longer is accorded existence, as it has mistakenly been from the Greek Atomist on down. Hence there is no void, no nothing; thus all things are seen literally to be connected, & there is what I saw that I called Valis. Not a plurality of atomized discontinuous pieces of matter but, rather, one interconnected whole. If the void (mēden) is accorded no reality — it is not regarded as existent — then it follows logically that by definition there is an literal continuum. This cannot be expressed or imagined if you premise the atoms & void discontinuous matter basis. But I did see it (3-74) because it is true, & when we see void we are deluded.

Thus, the comprehension of the Forms as the basis of reality (rather than emphasizing the Kantian ordering categories) leads to the experience of the 5D instead of 4D world. & this turns out finally, when all falsifications are abolished, to be the Eleatic continuum which is the ancient (extinct) true Kosmos; it is still there, but we are unable to see it. We suffer, then, a negative hallucination; we don't see what is not there. We fail to see what is.

Perhaps you see Kosmos — continuum — when the Kantian categories go because space, like time & causation, is one of them; then I'm saying that all three go: time, certaintly; that most of all. Causation, certaintly; I saw that go. & also space: discrete plurality gives way to unity because "space" equals "void" & is a Kantian category; which is to say "discontinuous matter" is connected with these categories & is false.

But the implication of the AI Voice's statement "...and it's still there" is simply that I saw because I saw world as it is, and since it is in itself true Kosmos, I therefore saw true Kosmos. In a way, no argument is needed to explain why you see something if indeed it is there. The problem is in explaining why, if it's there, we do not see it, and I trace this "not" to the Atomists. By utilizing the platonic forms instead of (or over and above) the Kantian ordering categories I probably abreacted in terms of phylogenic worldview to before the Atomists - which fits in with my distinct impression on 3-74 that I was seeing world "the way they saw it in the Middle Ages" and my conception of how they saw world in the Middle Ages is, in fact, more they way platonists see it (the archetype of e.g. rose that is outside of time printing out the many particulars Rose^1, Rose^2, Rose^3, etc).

Here is the point Im missing. The Form, (ie knowledge of the forms) is already in the human mind. This knowledge does not arise from experience [of world]. Plato is absolutely right: this knowledge is recovered as memory, not learned; that is, anamnesis occurs. This is what Plato is saying and this is what I am saying, therefore this is indeed what 2-74 and the meta-abstraction is all about. The knowledge (of the Forms) however is on the mind but in latent state; the mind does not know -remember- that it knows. My writing about the Form-axis in "Ubik" is not where I got the idea of the forms - it is an instance of my knowing a priori and in an innate way about the Forms. "Ubik" represents a partial, inadequate recovery of this innate knowledge in a purely theoretical way. There is as yet no application of

2-13 "a long extinct true cosmos + it's still There" implies far more - + indeed something other - than a subjective way by which the percipient organizes reality (as by the Kantian categories). What is stated is: this "true cosmos" has an objective existence but we are unable to apprehend it, + in fact are prevented from apprehending it, due to our faulty "dis‐ continuous matter" atomistic physics, So I did move than simply change the way I structure reality. This "true cosmos" is (as the AI voice put it) "still there"; it is There but from our civilization's standpoint extinct long extinct, in terms of both our theories about reality + our apperception of reality. What I did was retrieve what is There what is objectively real in itself - I saw correctly. Not our 2000 years of discontinuous matter physics is fallacious. + it occludes us to what is really there, + since "true cosmos" I by definition includes the percipient himself as part of this total, absolute whole, we are unable to know our own true nature as well as the true nature of reality. Thus the Q: "Ti to on?" has been answered wrang from Leucippus on - hence our inauthentic sein, hence Geworfenheit: estrangement ➀ In + by the 2-74 meta-abstraction the fundamental error of 2000 years of faulty physics was annihilated in an instant whereupon true reality - Parmenides' continuum phased back into palpability for me.
